rulings.Case Title: SHARON WOODY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Re-Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings;
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, NOVEMBER 13, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant Hanson Permanente Cement Inc.¿s motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted. No opposition filed. Hearing required unless counsel for defendant informs Mr. Woody of this tentative ruling and he states that he does not wish to contest. The hearing will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM MOORES VS. C.C. MOORE & CO. ENGINEERS*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO LOS ANGELES COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Transfer Venue To Los Angeles County Superior Court
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, NOVEMBER 13, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2, Defendant Calportland Co.¿s motion to transfer venue to the County of Los Angeles is granted. Plaintiff does not oppose defendant¿s motion. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN MCHUGH et al VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, NOVEMBER 13, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3, Defendant Macy¿s, Inc.¿s demurrer to plaintiffs¿ third amended complaint is overruled. Defendant¿s demurrer was not brought on grounds on which a demurrer may be based pursuant to C.C.P. 430.10. Defendant did not challenge the face of the complaint. Even if the court takes judicial notice of defendant¿s Exhibits C, D and E, they do not show that plaintiffs cannot state a cause of action against defendant. Defendant has 10 days to answer. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN PERNIZ VS. SEQUOIA VENTURES INC.
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, NOVEMBER 13, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 4, Defendant Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.¿s general demurrer to plaintiffs¿ second amended complaint is sustained with five days leave to amend to give plaintiffs an opportunity, if they are able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71. Plaintiffs did not substantively oppose defendant¿s demurrer. Although not cited by defendant, Bockrath controls. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
